free_text: Na-23, Xe-124,132,134 data with different monitor
CS value - 657+-16 mb for Au-197 MACS.
-Capture cross section measurements of krypton and
xenon isotopes and the fundamental parameters of the
S-process.
The Karlsruhe pulsed 3 MV Van de Graaff
accelerator was used.
Neutrons were produced near the threshold,
which gives a kinematically collimated neutron beam.
.The neutron spectrum was similar to a Maxwellian
spectrum with a temperature of 25 keV.
.Natural Kr and Xe was used as sample material. The
activation samples used were /1/ zeolite tablets, 6 mm
in diameter, loaded with 52.7 cm3 Kr per gram of
sample, sandwiched between two Au foils of the same
diameter or an Au foil between two zeolite tablets,
/2/ Na4-Xe-O6*8(H2O) tablet,6mm in diameter,sandwiched
between two Au foils, /3/ a pressurized gas sample
with a mixture of Kr and Xe gas in a stainless steel
sphere, 20 mm in diameter, 0.5 mm wall thickness. For
background determination, an empty stainless steel
sphere was used.
Simultaneous activation of target material and
standard Au foils. A fast cyclic activation system
with a fast sample changer operating with compressed
air was used. Both the irradiation and counting phases
were adjusted to be 38 sec.
The activity was counted with a GeLi-detector,
with a relative efficiency of 28 percent and an energy
resolution of 2.4 keV at 1.33 MeV. To obtain a fast
cyclic time, the detector was placed close to the
neutron generation. It was therefore well shielded by
lead and Li-loaded paraffin. During the activity
counting phase the neutron generation was stopped by
a proton beam stop in the beam line.
The neutron flux was monitored by a Li-6 glass
scintillation counter.
Intensity 95.52+-0.06 %.
For background.
The errors are from statistics, gamma-ray
intensity per decay, GeLi-detector efficiency, sample
weight, relative isotopic abundance, gamma-ray
attenuation, and the Au-197 standard.
Statistical uncertainty :
large as 3% to 4.8% for Xe-136 and Xe-126(n,g)Xe-127m,
respectively.
Uncertainty in the relative isotopic
abundance is small except for Xe-124 and Xe-126, where
Ge(Li) efficiency uncertainty 2%.
Uncertainty in the gamma-ray attenuation
for pressured gas sample was estimated to be 4.%.
Data taken from Table 2 of J,AJ,375,823,1991.
